steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the poem. The key signal is the opening: 'My spirit swoons, and all my senses cry / For Ocean's breast.' The speaker consistently yearns for the ocean to bring sleep and peace.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — speaker's doubt about finding peace: The speaker expresses longing, not doubt. The tone is wistful desire, not uncertainty. ✗
  • Choice B — contrast between toil and peace as the main point: That contrast is a supporting detail, not the central purpose. ✗
  • Choice C — escalating emotional intensity: The poem's emotional register is steady longing, not an escalating buildup. ✗
  • Choice D — speaker's longing for ocean-born tranquility: Directly captures the consistent emotional focus of the poem. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
